definition: zu — without doing;not doing
彼は何も言わずに出て行った。 — He left without saying anything.
nanka — something like; somehow; kind of
なんか変な音がする。 — I hear some strange sound.
wake — reason; meaning; circumstances
彼が来ないわけがない。 — There's no way he won't come.
beki — should; ought to
もっと勉強するべきだ。 — You should study more.
ze — (sentence-ending particle, expressing assertion/exclamation)
行くぜ！ — Let's go!
nashi — without;none;no
理由なしに欠席した。 — I was absent without reason.
nite — at, in, by (formal)
会議は東京にて開催されます。 — The meeting will be held in Tokyo.
ni yori — by, due to, according to
天候不良により、試合は中止された。 — Due to bad weather, the game was canceled.
nomi — only;alone
会員のみ入場できます。 — Only members can enter.
koso — indeed, precisely
今度こそ成功する。 — This time, I will succeed for sure.
tomo — even if, together with, both
彼とともに行く。 — I will go with him.
matome — summary;compilation
会議のまとめを作成した。 — I created a summary of the meeting.
ni oite — in; at; on (formal/written)
この点において、意見が異なります。 — Opinions differ on this point.
nao — furthermore, still, yet
なお、詳細は後日連絡します。 — Furthermore, details will be provided later.
tsutsu — pipe;tube
この筒は空です。 — This pipe is empty.
sorezore — each, respectively
それぞれの意見を述べた。 — Each stated their opinion.
shikamo — moreover, furthermore
彼は頭が良く、しかも優しい。 — He is smart, and moreover, kind.
sae — even; if only
子供でさえ知っている。 — Even a child knows.
kke — (particle indicating recollection or confirmation)
彼の名前、何だっけ？ — What was his name again?
yare — Oh dear; good grief
やれやれ、また宿題か。 — Oh dear, homework again.
oppai — breasts;boobies (informal)
赤ちゃんがおっぱいを飲んでいる。 — The baby is drinking milk from the breast.
chinamini — by the way;incidentally
ちなみに、彼は独身です。 — By the way, he is single.
sasuga — as expected;indeed;truly
さすがプロだね。 — As expected of a professional.
toitta — such as; like; for example
りんごやバナナといった果物が好きです。 — I like fruits such as apples and bananas.
soredemo — nevertheless; even so; however
彼は疲れていた。それでも、仕事を続けた。 — He was tired. Nevertheless, he continued working.
nasaru — to do (honorific)
社長は何をなさいますか？ — What will the president do?
o' — Oh! / Hey! (surprise/realization)
おっ、見て！虹が出てるよ。 — Oh, look! There's a rainbow.
izure — eventually;sooner or later;in due course
いずれ、この問題は解決するでしょう。 — Eventually, this problem will be resolved.
o — Oh! (informal, often used in text)
ぉ、そうなんだ。 — Oh, I see.
fu — (sighing sound, thinking sound)
ふう、やっと終わった。 — Phew, it's finally over.
aruiwa — or, alternatively
コーヒーあるいは紅茶、どちらにしますか？ — Coffee or tea, which would you like?
kashira — I wonder if, perhaps
明日、雨が降るかしら。 — I wonder if it will rain tomorrow.
sura — even
彼は自分の名前すら書けない。 — He can't even write his own name.
monono — although, though, but
彼は若いものの、とても経験豊富だ。 — Although he is young, he is very experienced.
dara — probably, right? (informal)
明日も晴れるだら。 — It'll probably be sunny tomorrow too, right?
tadashi — however; provided that
参加は自由です。ただし、事前登録が必要です。 — Participation is free. However, pre-registration is required.
masani — exactly;precisely;truly
それはまさに私が言いたかったことです。 — That is exactly what I wanted to say.
masaka — no way;impossible;you don't say
まさか彼がそんなことをするとは。 — No way he would do such a thing.
to tomo ni — together with; along with; as (something happens)
彼は家族とともに旅行した。 — He traveled together with his family.
tan — charcoal
暖炉に炭をくべた。 — I put charcoal in the fireplace.
moshikuwa — or; otherwise
電話、もしくはメールでご連絡ください。 — Please contact us by phone or email.
marude — as if;as though;completely
まるで夢のようだ。 — It's just like a dream.
katsute — once;formerly
かつてこの場所には大きな木がありました。 — Once, there was a large tree in this place.
nantoka — somehow;in some way
なんとか間に合った。 — I somehow made it in time.
mushiro — rather;instead
彼は怒るどころか、むしろ笑っていた。 — Far from being angry, he was rather laughing.
amari ni — too much;excessively
彼の態度はあまりにひどかった。 — His attitude was too terrible.
omae — you (informal, often rude)
おまえ、何してるんだ？ — What are you doing?
kuse — habit;peculiar habit;tendency
彼は爪を噛むくせがある。 — He has a habit of biting his nails.
darake — full of;covered in;riddled with
彼の部屋はゴミだらけだ。 — His room is full of trash.
sonomono — itself;the very thing
彼は優しさそのものだ。 — He is kindness itself.
yabai — dangerous;awful;amazing;cool (colloquial)
この状況はやばい。 — This situation is dangerous.
kan — clang; ding (sound of metal)
鐘がかんと鳴った。 — The bell rang with a clang.
domo — (informal/humble suffix, often for greetings or pluralizing people)
どうも、ども。 — Thanks, everyone. (very informal)
arayuru — all;every possible
あらゆる可能性を考える。 — Consider all possibilities.
gachi — apt to;prone to;tend to
彼は病気がちだ。 — He is prone to illness.
bakkari — only;just;nothing but (colloquial)
彼は遊んでばっかりいる。 — He's always just playing.
kire — Wow, beautiful! (exclamation)
わあ、きれ！ — Wow, beautiful!
douse — anyway;in any case;after all
どうせ無理だからやめよう。 — It's impossible anyway, so let's stop.
maji — seriously?; really? (colloquial)
まじで？信じられない。 — Seriously? I can't believe it.
iwayuru — what is called;so-called
いわゆる「クールジャパン」現象。 — The so-called "Cool Japan" phenomenon.
gan — cancer
彼はがんで亡くなった。 — He died of cancer.
tondemo — no way; unbelievable; not at all (informal, truncated)
とんでも！そんなことないよ。 — No way! That's not true.
Wazawaza — Expressly;Specially;To go out of one's way
わざわざ来てくれてありがとう。 — Thank you for coming all this way.
Wazuka — Only a little;A mere
わずかな時間しかなかった。 — There was only a little time.
Sekkaku — With much trouble;At great pains;After all the trouble
せっかく来たのに、雨が降ってきた。 — After coming all this way, it started to rain.
Youyaku — At last;Finally
ようやく宿題が終わった。 — I finally finished my homework.
Motomoto — Originally;From the start
彼はもともとエンジニアだった。 — He was originally an engineer.
Sassato — Quickly;Promptly;Without delay
さっさと仕事を終わらせよう。 — Let's finish the work quickly.
Matomo — Decent;Proper;Respectable
彼はまともな人だ。 — He is a decent person.
tsuiteru — to be lucky;to be attached
今日はついてるね！ — You're lucky today!
mottomo — most;indeed;however
もっとも重要なのは健康だ。 — The most important thing is health.
mechakucha — messy;absurd;extremely
部屋がめちゃくちゃだ。 — The room is a mess.
kakawaru — to be involved in;to have to do with
その問題にはかかわりたくない。 — I don't want to be involved in that problem.
shin — core;heart;truth
この話のしんは何ですか？ — What is the core of this story?
makuru — to roll up;to do excessively;repeatedly
彼は食べまくった。 — He ate a lot (excessively).
fuzake — joke;prank;fooling around
そんなふざけはやめなさい。 — Stop such fooling around.
ni kakete — over (a period); concerning; from...to...
彼はスポーツにかけては天才だ。 — He is a genius when it comes to sports.
kōshita — such;this kind of
こうした問題はよくある。 — Such problems are common.
tatte — even if; even though
どんなに頑張ったって、無理なものは無理だ。 — Even if you try your best, what's impossible is impossible.
tatoe — even if; for example
たとえ雨が降っても、試合は行われる。 — Even if it rains, the game will be held.
haruka — far off;distant;by far
はるか遠くに山が見える。 — Mountains can be seen far off in the distance.
dōnika — somehow;in some way or another
どうにか間に合った。 — I somehow made it in time.
ossan — middle-aged man;old man (informal;sometimes derogatory)
あのおっさんはいつも公園で寝ている。 — That old man is always sleeping in the park.
goku — very;extremely;quite
これはごく一般的なことです。 — This is a very common thing.
iyoiyo — at last;finally;all the more
いよいよ出発の日が来た。 — Finally, the day of departure has come.
dōyara — it seems;apparently;somehow
どうやら彼はもう帰ったようだ。 — It seems he has already gone home.
yoreru — to be able to approach;to be able to drop by
駅に寄れる店はありますか？ — Is there a shop where I can drop by near the station?
ittai — what on earth;what in the world
いったい何が起こったんだ？ — What on earth happened?
sorenari — as it is;in its own way;accordingly
彼はそれなりに頑張っている。 — He is doing his best in his own way.
toka iu — something like; they say
彼は「疲れた」とか言っていた。 — He was saying something like "I'm tired."
kōshite — thus;in this way
こうして、私たちは友達になった。 — Thus, we became friends.
tamatama — by chance;accidentally
たまたま彼に会った。 — I met him by chance.
chai — (contraction of ~te shimau, indicating completion or regret)
宿題を忘れちゃった。 — I forgot my homework (regrettably).
rō — wax
蝋でろうそくを作る。 — I make candles with wax.
semete — at least;at the very least
せめて謝るべきだ。 — You should at least apologize.
samazama — various;diverse
さまざまな意見が出た。 — Various opinions were expressed.
akumade — to the last;to the end;after all
それはあくまで私の意見です。 — That is just my opinion, after all.
choi — a little;a bit (informal)
ちょいと待ってください。 — Please wait a bit.
son — such; that kind of (informal shortening of そんな)
そんことないよ。 — That's not true.
anma — not much;not very (informal shortening of あまり)
あんま好きじゃない。 — I don't like it much.
sore ni shitemo — even so; nevertheless
彼は遅刻したが、それにしても仕事は速い。 — He was late, but even so, his work is fast.
hitasura — earnestly;solely;intently
彼はひたすら走り続けた。 — He continued to run intently.
tokoroga — however; even so; despite that
彼は来るはずだった。ところが来なかった。 — He was supposed to come. However, he didn't.
dokoroka — far from; on the contrary; let alone
彼は手伝うどころか、邪魔をした。 — Far from helping, he got in the way.
tomokaku — anyhow;anyway;at any rate
ともかく、やってみましょう。 — Anyway, let's try it.
kashi — I wonder; perhaps (often as a particle)
明日は晴れるかしら。 — I wonder if it will be sunny tomorrow.
masumasu — increasingly;more and more
彼女はますます美しくなった。 — She became more and more beautiful.
nantomu — nothing;not at all;indeed;utterly
なんとも言えない気持ちです。 — It's an indescribable feeling.
sokosoko — reasonably;fairly;so-so
彼の日本語はそこそこ話せます。 — He can speak Japanese reasonably well.
ppanashi — leaving (something) on;open;as is (often as a suffix)
テレビをつけっぱなしで寝てしまった。 — I fell asleep with the TV left on.
yagate — soon;before long;eventually
やがて夜が明けるだろう。 — Soon the night will break.
nja — well then; see ya (informal contraction of それでは)
んじゃ、また明日。 — Well then, see you tomorrow.
sokora — around there;that sort of place
そこらにある店で何か買おう。 — Let's buy something at a shop around there.
yatara — excessively;indiscriminately
彼はやたらと質問する。 — He asks an excessive number of questions.
dano — and so on; and the like (listing examples)
彼は文句だの不平だのばかり言っている。 — He's always complaining and grumbling and the like.
kodawari — obsession;particularity;commitment
彼は品質に強いこだわりがある。 — He has a strong commitment to quality.
jikkuri — carefully;thoroughly;patiently
じっくり考えてから決めてください。 — Please decide after thinking carefully.
toaru — a certain;a particular
とある町で不思議な出来事が起こった。 — A strange event happened in a certain town.
ni tsuki — (grammatical expression: per, due to)
部屋の清掃につき、ご協力ください。 — Please cooperate due to room cleaning.
chimau — (colloquial contraction of てしまう)
忘れちまった。 — I forgot (it completely/unintentionally).
akirame — resignation;giving up;despair
彼はあきらめが早い。 — He gives up quickly.
ate — aim;purpose;expectation;reliance
彼の言葉は当てにならない。 — His words are unreliable.
shikashinagara — however; nevertheless; but
彼は努力した。しかしながら、成功しなかった。 — He tried hard. However, he did not succeed.
masaka no — unbelievable; unexpected; by some chance
まさかの展開に驚いた。 — I was surprised by the unexpected turn of events.
ichiichi — one by one;in detail;fussily
いちいち文句を言うな。 — Don't complain about every little thing.
ochi — punchline;ending
その話の落ちが面白かった。 — The punchline of that story was funny.
ni watatte — throughout; over (a period/range)
彼は数年にわたって研究を続けた。 — He continued his research over several years.
mamire — covered in; smeared with
彼は泥まみれになった。 — He got covered in mud.
manma — as is;just as it is (colloquial)
そのまんまにしておいて。 — Leave it as it is.
yarakasu — to mess up;to make a blunder (colloquial)
また大きな失敗をやらかしてしまった。 — I messed up big time again.
dakara koso — that's precisely why; for that very reason
難しいからこそ、挑戦する価値がある。 — That's precisely why it's worth challenging.
gatai — difficult to; hard to
この問題は理解しがたい。 — This problem is difficult to understand.
souiya — Speaking of which; Now that you mention it
そういや、田中さん、最近見ないね。 — Speaking of which, I haven't seen Tanaka-san lately.
shibashiba — often;frequently
彼はしばしば図書館に行く。 — He often goes to the library.
tamaranai — unbearable;irresistible
暑くてたまらない。 — It's unbearably hot.
mare — rare;uncommon
この種類の花はまれだ。 — This type of flower is rare.
nandakanda — one way or another;for one reason or another
なんだかんだ言って、彼はいつも助けてくれる。 — One way or another, he always helps me.
monosugoi — tremendous;terrible;amazing
ものすごいスピードで走る。 — To run at a tremendous speed.
yoso — another place;elsewhere;stranger
よその家に行く。 — To go to another's house.
zure — gap;discrepancy;shift
計画にずれが生じた。 — A discrepancy occurred in the plan.
ppuri — (suffix) manner;way;state
彼の食べっぷりはすごい。 — His eating manner is amazing.
assari — easily;simply;lightly (food)
彼はその問題をあっさり解決した。 — He easily solved the problem.
akan — no good;useless;can't (Kansai dialect)
それはあかん。 — That's no good.
gurumi — (suffix) entire;whole;together with
家族ぐるみで旅行に行った。 — We went on a trip as a whole family.
suri — pickpocket
電車の中で財布をすりに盗まれた。 — My wallet was stolen by a pickpocket on the train.
haya — dace (fish)
川でハヤを釣った。 — I caught dace in the river.
ikanaru — any kind of;whatever
いかなる困難にも立ち向かう。 — I will face any kind of difficulty.
imadani — still;even now
彼は未だにそのことを信じている。 — He still believes that.
shitagatte — therefore, consequently
彼は病気だ。したがって、学校を休む。 — He is sick. Therefore, he will be absent from school.
zuke — pickled item, marinated item
この漬けは美味しい。 — This pickled item is delicious.
ni atatte — at the time of, on the occasion of
開会にあたって、一言ご挨拶申し上げます。 — On the occasion of the opening, I would like to say a few words.
mie — show;display;vanity
彼は見栄を張るのが好きだ。 — He likes to show off.
unzari — fed up;bored
毎日同じ仕事でうんざりする。 — I'm fed up with the same work every day.
kicchiri — precisely;exactly
彼は時間をきっちり守る。 — He is punctual.
za — seat;place
彼は座に着いた。 — He took his seat.
kaette — on the contrary;rather
急いだらかえって遅くなった。 — I hurried, but on the contrary, I became late.
akumademo — to the last;after all;strictly
それはあくまでも私の意見です。 — That is strictly my opinion.
kuda — pipe;tube
水道管のくだが壊れて水漏れしている。 — The water pipe is broken and leaking.
kori — stiffness;lump;knot
肩のこりがひどい。 — I have severe stiffness in my shoulders.
tomo ni — together with;along with
彼らは困難をともに乗り越えた。 — They overcame difficulties together.
poi — -ish;-like;tending to
彼は子供っぽいところがある。 — He has a childish side.
ayumi — walk;progress;history
会社のあゆみを振り返る。 — We look back at the company's progress.
hitomi — pupil (of the eye);eye
彼女のひとみはとても美しい。 — Her eyes are very beautiful.
akane — madder;deep red
夕焼け空があかね色に染まる。 — The sunset sky is dyed madder red.
isso — rather;sooner;might as well
いっそやめてしまおうか。 — Should I just quit?
kkiri — only, just, since (doing)
彼とは一度会ったっきりだ。 — I've only met him once.
meguru — to go around;to circulate
地球は太陽の周りを巡る。 — The Earth revolves around the sun.
guchi — complaint;grumble
彼はいつも愚痴ばかり言っている。 — He's always complaining.
ni atari — at the time of, on the occasion of
開会にあたり、一言ご挨拶申し上げます。 — On the occasion of the opening, I would like to say a few words.
konasu — to manage;to handle;to master
彼は仕事をうまくこなす。 — He handles his work well.
komoru — to seclude oneself;to be confined;to be filled with
部屋にこもって勉強する。 — I seclude myself in my room to study.
momu — to rub;to massage;to knead
肩をもむ。 — Massage the shoulders.
arakajime — beforehand;in advance
あらかじめ予約しておく。 — Book in advance.
arasu — to lay waste;to devastate;to ransack
泥棒が家を荒らした。 — The thief ransacked the house.
yutori — room;space;leeway
時間にゆとりがない。 — I don't have enough time (leeway).
ikanimo — indeed;truly;as expected
いかにも彼らしい答えだ。 — That's indeed an answer typical of him.
seizei — at most;at best;to the utmost
彼はせいぜい1時間しか待てない。 — He can wait at most for one hour.
tsute — connections;influence;means
彼はつてを頼って仕事を見つけた。 — He found a job by relying on his connections.
teko — lever;crowbar
てこを使って重い岩を動かした。 — I moved the heavy rock using a lever.
dounimo — cannot possibly;no matter how hard one tries
どうにもならない問題だ。 — It's a problem that cannot possibly be solved.
niwatari — throughout;over (a period;range)
彼は数年にわたり研究を続けた。 — He continued his research over several years.
matomaru — to be collected;to be settled;to be unified
意見がまとまった。 — The opinions were settled.
megumi — blessing;grace;favor
自然の恵みに感謝する。 — We are grateful for nature's blessings.
tottoto — quickly;promptly;without delay (often imperative)
とっとと帰れ！ — Go home quickly!
kui — pile;stake;post
彼は地面に杭を打ち込んだ。 — He drove a stake into the ground.
kodawaru — to be particular about;to be fastidious;to stick to
彼は品質にこだわる。 — He is particular about quality.
sayaka — clear;bright;distinct
さやかな月明かり。 — Clear moonlight.
yoppodo — considerably;really;very
よっぽど疲れていたのだろう。 — He must have been considerably tired.
saranaru — further;additional
さらなる情報が必要です。 — Further information is needed.
tadatada — simply;merely;solely
彼はただただ驚いていた。 — He was simply surprised.
tokoton — to the end;thoroughly;to the utmost
とことん話し合おう。 — Let's discuss it thoroughly.
Yossha — Alright! / Yes!
よっしゃ、これで終わりだ！ — Alright, this is the end!
Komi — crowd;congestion;inclusion
この電車はいつもこみがひどい。 — This train is always very crowded.
Sashi — ruler;pointer;sashimi (cut)
定規をさしと呼ぶこともあります。 — Sometimes a ruler is called a sashi.
Tekkiri — surely;certainly;I was sure that...
てっきり彼は来ると思っていたのに。 — I was sure he would come, but he didn't.
Hatena — what?; huh?; question mark
はてな、どうしたんだろう？ — Huh, what happened?
Shitsukoi — persistent;nagging;greasy (food)
彼のしつこい勧誘に困っている。 — I'm troubled by his persistent solicitations.
Tsubuyaki — murmur;whisper;tweet
彼のつぶやきが聞こえた。 — I heard his murmur.
Toriwake — especially;above all;particularly
彼はとりわけ数学が得意だ。 — He is especially good at mathematics.
Shocchū — always;constantly;frequently
彼はしょっちゅう遅刻する。 — He is always late.
taishita — great;significant;considerable (often used with negative connotation)
たいしたことないよ。 — It's not a big deal.
marugoto — whole;entirely;all
彼はリンゴをまるごと食べた。 — He ate the apple whole.
omotenashi — hospitality;entertainment
日本のおもてなしは素晴らしいです。 — Japanese hospitality is wonderful.
kichi — base; fortress
軍事基地は厳重に警備されている。 — The military base is heavily guarded.
kagiri — limit;extent;as long as
私の知る限り、彼は正直な人です。 — As far as I know, he is an honest person.
gake — cliff;precipice
その家は崖の近くに建っている。 — That house is built near a cliff.
todome — finishing blow;coup de grâce
彼がとどめを刺した。 — He delivered the finishing blow.
nimatsuwaru — to be associated with;to be related to
その伝説にまつわる話はたくさんある。 — There are many stories associated with that legend.
karaniwa — now that, since, as long as
やると決めたからには、最後までやり遂げよう。 — Now that we've decided to do it, let's see it through to the end.
chimae — (do something) completely/regrettably (colloquial imperative)
早く食べちまえ！ — Eat it up quickly!
tsuto — diligently;assiduously;quickly
彼はつとめて仕事に励んだ。 — He diligently applied himself to his work.
masashiku — surely;undoubtedly;exactly
それはまさしく彼の作品だ。 — That is undoubtedly his work.
shikkuri — perfectly;snugly;smoothly
この服は私にしっくりくる。 — These clothes fit me perfectly.
jiwajiwa — slowly but steadily;gradually
痛みがじわじわと広がった。 — The pain spread slowly but steadily.
honoka — faint;subtle;dim
ほのかな光が差し込んだ。 — A faint light shone in.
moro — completely;directly;entirely
